opp.ais_why:
• Christopher Tandu's intro framed Symaplus's need as a holistic CLM to replace Excel reminders and SharePoint storage - the 13-minute call surfaced no Merlin, agentic, or GenAI language from either side.
• RFI is ~98% CLM-focused, targeting ERP integration with Dynamics/SAP/Oracle; AI was never raised as a requirement.
• Single short intro recorded; opp is in RFI-await mode with no demo yet, so the absence of AI cues likely reflects early stage rather than resistance.

north_star_verdict.headline: 81 days to close with zero buyer engagement in 7 months, single-threaded to an operational contact with no power, no MEDDPICC elements confirmed, and a stalled RFI process that was promised Dec 2025 but never issued. Rep reached out 19 Jun awaiting reply but this is a rep-side touch, not buyer engagement. Deal is effectively dormant.

flags: RE-ENGAGE the relevant stakeholder STATUS: 7-month stall since the 24 Nov 2025 discovery call; rep checked in 19 Jun (no buyer reply yet). The next immediate move (act by 2 Jul, within 7 days) is for the deal team to call Christopher Tandu directly (phone, not email) to confirm: (a) was the RFI issued and to how many vendors, (b) has CIMA shortlisted vendors and is Zycus on the shortlist, (c) what is Agiloft's status (demos run, finalist position), and (d) what is the target date for demos and RFP. Without this confirmation Zycus is flying blind—cannot confirm it is still in consideration, cannot position against Agiloft, and cannot plan the next milestone. If Christopher does not respond by 2 Jul, escalate to the deal owner's manager (executive connect) to attempt a senior-level re-entry via one of the finance or procurement execs (Eric Van Acker, David Gimenez) by 9 Jul.

MULTI-THREAD TO the relevant stakeholder THE ECONOMIC BUYER: Christopher Tandu is an operational procurement specialist with no budget authority—he reports to 'the board and the sponsor' and 'the committee' (all unnamed). The evaluation is committee-driven but Zycus has zero visibility into who controls the CLM budget, who signs the contract, or who the exec sponsor is. The account-level contact list includes two senior finance leaders (Eric Van Acker, Senior VP Finance; Greg Weiderman, Sr. VP Finance & Operations) and a procurement director (David Gimenez)—any could be the Economic Buyer, but none are confirmed as involved. value.flag: DIFFERENTIATE ON DYNAMICS + MERLIN BEFORE THE DEMO: Agiloft is the named competitor but the competitive wedge is unmapped—no calls, tasks, or fields compare Zycus vs Agiloft on fit, and Christopher has not stated his preference or Agiloft's strengths/weaknesses. Zycus confirmed two differentiators on the 24 Nov call that were not activated: (1) out-of-the-box Microsoft Dynamics ERP integration with in-house delivery (vs third-party integrators), which directly addresses Christopher's stated requirement and the recent Dynamics deployment, and (2) Merlin AI for contract intelligence and renewal automation, which directly addresses the 20% surprise-increase pain and the 600-PM decentralized-tracking challenge (but Merlin was never discussed with the buyer despite being in Products scope). The next move (act by 19 Jul, before any demo) is to send Christopher a one-page 'How Zycus Solves CIMA's Top 3 CLM Challenges' brief that ties Dynamics integration time-to-value and Merlin renewal intelligence directly to his stated pain points (20% increases, manual Excel tracking, 600 PMs with no standard), and explicitly contrasts these capabilities with typical vendor approaches (e.g. third-party integration delays, manual contract alerting). Then ask: do these two capabilities rank in your top 3 decision criteria vs Agiloft, and should we feature them in the demo? This activates the wedge before the competitive bake-off and tests whether it resonates.
